content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 14, 1989

The Honorable Jerry Eaves
Member of the Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-89-109 189

Dear Mr. Eaves:

You have requested advice regarding application of the mass
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1/, as
amended by Proposition 73 on the June 7, 1988 ballot.

QUESTION

May you send out at public expense several thousand postcards
announcing two "seniorfairs" at which you will speak and which you
are sponsoring for seniors?

CONCLUSION

A mass mailing of the postcards may not be sent at public
expense so long as the text of the postcards makes reference to
you.  If the text does not make reference to you, the mailing may
include your name in the return address if it is sent on your
standard letterhead, forms or envelopes.

FACTS

You have asked whether you may send a postcard at public
expense to several thousand constituents.  The postcard announces
two separate events which you are sponsoring for seniors.  You
will be present at both events to talk to seniors and to let them
know about your legislative program.

_____________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

The Honorable Jerry Eaves
April 14, 1989
Page 2

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 provides that no mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense. The term "mass mailing" means over two hundred
substantially similar pieces of mail. (Section 82041.5.)

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) clarifies that the purpose
of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using public
funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings.
Specifically, a mass mailing may not be sent at public expense if
it is sent by an elected officer and includes the name, office,
photograph or other reference to the elected officer on the
document. (Regulation 18901 (c)(2)(A)(ii).) Subdivision (f) of
the regulation lists several exemptions from the prohibition, two
of which are relevant here.

Subdivision (f)(8) exempts:

     (8) Mailings sent to the elected officer's
constituents which directly relate to that elected
official's incumbent governmental duties and which
solely include the time, date, place, and a concise
description of the subject matter of a public meeting to
be held by the elected officer;

Subdivision (f)(10) exempts:

     (10) Announcements of the time, date, place, and
subject matter of official agency events which include a
listing of elected officers and others who will
participate in the events.

The postcard you have sent us (copy attached) includes your
name in its text. Accordingly, unless it falls under one of the
listed exemptions, it may not be sent in a mass mailing at public
expense.

We do not believe the postcard falls under either of the
exemptions. The exemptions are to be narrowly construed.
(Lancaster Advice Letter, No. I-89-026; Clark Advice Letter, No.
I-89-031; Kurie Advice Letter, No. A-89-099; copies enclosed.)
The exemption in subdivision (f)(8) applies to notices of a public
meeting to be held by the elected officer. The postcard notifies
constituents about the "seniorfairs" at which "more than 70
experts" will be available to answer questions on various issues
of interest to seniors. The seniorfairs will be held on two
different days from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. The postcard then
mentions that you will also be there to talk about your
legislative program, and issues of concern to the Legislature.
This is not the type of notice to which the exemption applies.
The "seniorfairs" are much more than public meetings at which you
will enter into an exchange with your constituents relating to
your incumbent governmental duties. They are informational events

The Honorable Jerry Eaves
April 14, 1989
Page 3

at which seniors will be able to obtain information about issues
from a variety of individuals and sources. For example, the
notice provides that "[M]ore than 70 experts will be available to
answer your questions on issues such as catastrophic health laws,
crime prevention, campaign programs, government benefits, fitness,
free health screenings, and much more."

The postcard also fails to qualify as an announcement of an
official agency event as described in subdivision (f)(10). This
event is not sponsored by the Legislature. Furthermore, the
postcard contains information beyond that allowed under the
exemption.

The postcards may be sent at public expense if there is no
reference made to you in the text. Your name may appear in the
return address if the mailing is sent on your standard letterhead,
forms or envelopes. (Mott-Smith Advice Letter, No. A-89-153;
Gentry Advice Letter No. A-89-166; copies enclosed.)

I trust this answers your question. If you have any further
questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:ld

Enclosure

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 31, 1989


Honorable Jerry Eaves
Assemblymember, Sixty-Sixth District
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-189

Dear Assemblyman Eaves:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on March 30, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
State Capitol
Sacramento
California 95814
(916) 445-4843

DISTRICT OFFICE
224 N. Riverside Avenue
Suite A
Rialto, California 92376
(714) 820-1902

Assembly

COMMITTEES:
FINANCE AND INSURANCE
CHAIRMAN, UNEMPLOYMENT
AND DISABILITY
INSURANCE SUBCOMMITTEE
TRANSPORTATION
CHAIRMAN, CALIFORNIA
HIGHWAY PATROL
SUBCOMMITTEE
UTILITIES AND COMMERCE
WAYS & MEANS
CHAIRMAN, SELECT
COMMITTEE ON AVIATION

California Legislature

JERRY EAVES
ASSISTANT MAJORITY FLOOR LEADER
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY SIXTH DISTRICT

REPRESENTING THE CITIES AND COMMUNITIES OF RIALTO,
SAN BERNARDINO, FONTANA, COLTON, ONTARIO, BLOOMINGTON, AND LAKE ARROWHEAD

March 30, 1989

Mr. John H. Larson
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Larson:

     This is to request an FPPC opinion as to the legality of
mailing a postcard at government expense to several thousand
constituents. The postcard announces two separate events which I
am sponsoring for seniors and will be attended by experts on
issues affecting the elderly. I will be present at
both events to talk to seniors about the issues which are
important to them and to let them know about my legislative
program.

     The text of the postcard in enclosed.

     In order to meet time-line requirements, I would like your
reply within 10 working days.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        JERRY EAVES

JE:deb

Enclosure

MAR 30 '89 12:31 EHVES                                                                                    P.4


                    Celebrate Older Americans Month
                    at the Second Annual Seniorfairs!

More than 70 experts will be available to answer your questions
on issues such as catastrophic health laws, crime prevention,
companion programs, government benefits, fitness, free health
screenings, and much more.

Assemblyman Terry Eaves will be there to talk with you about his
legislative program, issues of concern to the Legislature and to
answer any questions you may have about these issues.

                    Learn what services are available for you!

Friday, May 12th, 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.
Peter Lague Center, 292 "O" Street, Colton

Friday, May 19th, 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.
Steelworkers Oldtimers Centre, 16707 Marygold, Fontana

Please call 820-1902 no later than May 2 for reservations.

expense so long as the text of the postcards makes reference to
